task.title: Reduce canonical surface and projection write amplification
task.summary: Projection firewall first slice completed: canonical/generated contract documented and refresh=false paths now avoid projection locks/rebuilds where safe.

goal.outcome: Normal CodeWiki updates write only canonical truth while generated graph/status/lint/roadmap views are rebuilt as disposable projections.
acceptance: Canonical and generated file classes are documented in system knowledge.
Generated projection files are marked or described as tool-owned/read-only for agents.
At least one mutator path avoids unnecessary generated projection writes unless refresh is explicitly requested.
codewiki_state still returns current status after refresh.
non_goals: Remove all generated files from the repository.
Rewrite the whole roadmap storage model.

delta.desired: Agents see a small canonical write surface and projection files become generated, tool-owned caches.
delta.current: Task/session workflows can rewrite multiple roadmap/state/graph/lint/status projection files, increasing token cost and drift surface.
delta.closure: Projection firewall documented and first write-amplification reduction implemented.

acceptance_matrix.criterion: Canonical and generated file classes are documented in system knowledge.

non_goals: Remove all generated files from the repository.

recent_evidence.title: Updated 1 roadmap task(s)
recent_evidence.summary: TASK-012 Reduce canonical surface and projection write amplification

recent_evidence.title: Reduce canonical surface and projection write amplification
recent_evidence.summary: Implemented projection firewall first slice: documented canonical/generated file classes, made session mutator skip projection rebuilds, and made roadmap mutation locks omit generated target paths when refresh=false.
issues: codewiki_task still defaults refresh=true for compatibility because close/archive retention currently depends on rebuild side effects; callers can use refresh=false for minimal writes.
